query_normalized: exploring the impact of data imputation methods on causal inference statistics
rationale: The paper focuses on a software framework for automating causal inference workflows and algorithm selection, but it does not measure, analyze, or provide empirical evidence regarding the specific impact of data imputation methods on causal inference statistics. It fails to satisfy any inclusion criteria (a-f) as it does not address the "data imputation" variable or the mechanism of how missing data handling affects causal estimates.
